g.text: Value score calculated as points divided by price

text.tspan: DATA-BACKED GUIDE TO RATINGS, PRICES & VALUE ACROSS CONNOISSEUR RATINGS

g.text: Based on WineEnthusiast.com review data; value is calculated from critic points and price.

tspan.style: font-style:normal;font-variant:normal;font-weight:bold;font-stretch:normal;font-family:Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;-inkscape-font-specification:'Georgia, Times New Roman, serif Bold'

text.tspan: HOW DO THE TYPES OF WINE COMPARE?
Critic scores range from 80 points to 100. Red wines have highest median score though the most quality variability.
The best rose is only scored at 96

g.text: WHAT CHARACTERISTICS ARE ASSOCIATED WITH BETTER WINE?
These are associations in reviewed wines, not universal rules :)
Columbia Valley, WA, United States · 100 pts · $80
Walla Walla Valley, WA, United States · 100 pts · $80
Napa Valley, CA, United States · 100 pts · $200
Vin Santo di Montepulcianoo, Italy · 100 pts · $210
High-scoring wines with strongest points-per-dollar score
Santa Ines 2000 Legado de Armida Reserva Chardonnay
Review data reflects WineEnthusiast.com (previously winemag.com) critic coverage. A select view <10% of Prices are
incomplete. Value_score is a simple points-per-dollar index. Wine style is inferred from variety (specific grape varietal) and
wine bottle title; ambiguous fortified or mixed wines are left as 'other.'
Data: WineEnthusiast.com (winemag.com) review data, via Kaggle
Companion project: The Global Wine Market -- A Data Portrait
